title: Fibonacci Sequence Generation with Incorrect Handling of Zero Input

statement.markdown: The Fibonacci sequence is a series of numbers where a number is the sum of the two preceding ones, usually starting with 0 and 1. Write a function that generates the Fibonacci sequence up to a given number of terms. However, the function should handle edge cases where the input is 0 or negative. The function should return an empty list in such cases.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a single integer n, where n is the number of terms in the Fibonacci sequence.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return a list of integers representing the Fibonacci sequence up to the nth term.
statement.notes: The function should use dynamic programming to generate the Fibonacci sequence efficiently.

constraints.description: Number of terms in the Fibonacci sequence

examples.explanation: Empty list for zero or negative input

hints.text: Use a bottom-up dynamic programming approach to generate the Fibonacci sequence efficiently.

editorial.title: Correct Fibonacci Sequence Generation
editorial.intuition: The key insight is to use a bottom-up dynamic programming approach to generate the Fibonacci sequence efficiently.
editorial.bruteForce: Naive recursive approach with high time complexity.
editorial.optimalSolution: Bottom-up dynamic programming approach with a single loop.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution has a time complexity of O(n) and produces the correct Fibonacci sequence.
editorial.dryRun: Test the solution with a small input to verify its correctness.
commonMistakes: Incorrect handling of zero or negative input
